overrideReason: Jul 17-18: Hormuz traffic remains near a standstill at just 10 transits (5 inbound, 5 outbound) in the 24 hours to Jul 17 per USNI News, down from a pre-crisis baseline of 88/day; the US naval blockade of Iranian ports imposed after the Jul 14 ceasefire collapse remains in force, Iran's IRGC says it stopped four vessels attempting a US-protected transit and struck two tankers with mines south of the strait on Jul 18 (a claim US CENTCOM disputes as false), and JMIC/UKMTO continue to rate the regional maritime threat SEVERE. Verified transit data lags several days behind.

reportedTransits.source: USNI News: Strait of Hormuz traffic hit a bottleneck of only 10 total transits (5 inbound, 5 outbound) in the 24 hours to Jul 17, under the US naval blockade reinstated Jul 14; 2 of the 5 inbound transits went dark and one bulk carrier reversed course before completing its crossing (prior data point: Kpler reported crossings falling to 8 on Jul 16, down from 15 on Jul 15)
